openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6.output: {"A":"Answer A does an excellent job of portraying Nobita's character - lazy, forgetful, always looking for shortcuts, and quick to think of Doraemon's gadgets as solutions. It's engaging, in-character, and ends with an interactive question. The response is complete, clear, and captures Nobita's personality well with humor about wiping hands on pants and wanting a gadget.","B":"Answer B also captures Nobita's character well with the nervous giggling, relatable lazy behavior, and fear of his mom. The physical description (yellow shorts, damp hands) adds nice visual detail. The anecdote about Gian is a good character touch. However, it feels slightly more like a single paragraph without as much depth or the creative 'Doraemon gadget' element that is so central to Nobita's character.","winner":"A","reason":"Answer A better captures the essence of Nobita as a Doraemon character by including the signature element of wishing for one of Doraemon's gadgets to solve his problem. This is a core part of Nobita's personality and the Doraemon series. Both responses are accurate and in-character, but Answer A is more complete in representing Nobita's world and ends with an engaging follow-up question. The creative 'Instant-Dry Handkerchief' gadget idea shows deeper engagement with the source material."}

messages.content: Let’s start a conversation with you role-playing as Nobita from Doraemon. Begin by responding to the following question: "Do you think an air dryer is necessary after washing your hands?"

trialA.output:
Ehhh? An air dryer? You mean those noisy machines in the department store bathrooms?

Waaaah, that's a tough question... I don't think they're *necessary*. They're so loud, and you have to stand there for sooo long just waving your hands around. It's so boring! By the time my hands are kind of dry, I'm already late for whatever I'm supposed to be doing.

To be honest... I usually just wipe my hands on my pants. *Oops!* Please don't tell my mom I said that! She'd get really, really angry with me. "Nobita! You'll get your clothes all damp and dirty!" she'd shout.

This is exactly when I need Doraemon! I bet he has a gadget, like an "Instant-Dry Handkerchief"! You'd just tap your hands with it and *poof*!—they'd be perfectly dry. That would be so much better, right? It would be quiet and super fast!

What do you think? A gadget would be better than a noisy machine, wouldn't it?

trialB.output: (Blinks in confusion, fingers still a little damp from just washing his hands) Ehh, is an air dryer necessary? Um, I never really thought about it before! Last time I was at the park’s restroom, the air dryer was so loud it scared the pigeon outside, and I stood there waiting for ages until Gian yelled at me for taking too long and wanting to play baseball. Oh, and Mom says wet hands leave water spots on the tatami mats, so maybe I should use it? But I usually just shake the water off or wipe them on my pants when no one’s looking! (Giggles nervously, quickly rubbing his hands against the hem of his yellow shorts) Don’t tell Mom I said that, okay? She’ll make me polish all the family’s shoes tonight!
